resources.string: Align the QR code within the frame
Camera permission is required to scan QR codes.
No servers yet. Tap + to import a config or subscription.
Paste a share link, URI, or subscription URL
Point the camera at a server QR code
Please fill in name and address
Pin Quick Connect or the Control Panel to your launcher
Status, live speed, and shortcuts to Servers and the app
Grant VPN permission in the app to use the widget
Long-press the home screen and choose Widgets → V2RayEz
Follow the system prompt to place the widget
Download the geo pack to route Iranian sites directly and cut latency.
Route Iranian sites and IPs directly (needs the geo pack)
Full device tunnel is on — App Proxy selections are ignored until you turn it off.
Allow-list is empty — all apps are routed except V2RayEz. Add apps or switch to bypass mode.
Defeat SNI-based DPI: scan for the best-performing SNI, then spoof / split / omit the TLS SNI and fragment the ClientHello.
Native · install ByeDPI in Core manager
libbyedpi.so is bundled. When enabled, the proxy dials through a local SOCKS5 that splits/fakes/reorders the ClientHello to defeat DPI.
libbyedpi.so is not bundled on this device, so desync can\'t run. The setting is saved and will activate once the native binary is present.
Front domain as SNI; real host in encrypted Host header
Using the bundled list of %1$d domains. Edit below to override.
One domain per line (leave empty for defaults)
Tap a result to use it as your SNI
Current SNI %1$s is not working — scan for a new one

Route Xray through a local dialer with fake-SNI probes and ClientHello fragmentation. The dialer TCP-connects to the server host/SNI IP (resolved on connect) so TLS can complete; fake SNI is only used for probes. Real TLS SNI stays on the server URI.
Local dialer accepts Xray and fronts via Cloudflare
Drops the dialer\'s cached fronting strategy so the next connect re-probes.
Embedded Tor core for whole-device or app routing
Greyed-out transports need their pack — install obfs4 / Snowflake / WebTunnel in Core manager.
Tor carries the full device or the selected server connection. Domain fronting is incompatible.
Use Tor for the full device or before a selected VLESS/VMESS/Trojan/Shadowsocks server
Download versions and pick the active binary
Default engine for servers set to “App default”
Not bundled in this APK for %1$s — download a release in Core manager
sing-box and mihomo ship download-only (not packaged in the APK) to keep the install small.
Per-app proxy changes apply after reconnecting the VPN
This device: %1$s — downloads match this CPU only
Tor + obfs4 (lyrebird) ship in the APK. Other pluggable transports & ByeDPI download on demand.
Full geoip + geosite databases power domain rules (ad-block, CN bypass, custom geosite rules). LAN bypass works without them.
Built-in mini set (LAN + CN IPs only) — install for full domain rules
Queued — will install when a release source is available
Check your connection and try again.
If your network blocks GitHub, connect to a server first — downloads route through the tunnel.
Xray uses the bundled AAR — download is optional/unused for VPN
Queued %1$s — publish `%2$s-<abi>.zip` on %3$s Releases, then Install again
Installed geo data (geoip %1$.1f MB, geosite %2$.1f MB) — applies on next connect
Deleted full geo data — mini geoip (LAN/CN) restored
No downloads yet — tap Download/Install on any pack above
Tor setup failed — try again or add bridges
Requires VPN permission. Routes all apps through Tor (Xray only). Conflicts with MITM capture.
Turn off MITM “Capture all apps” before enabling Tor whole-device tunnel
Tor “Tunnel whole device” is active. Disable it to connect to a VPN server.
Tor “Tunnel whole device” (or Tor) conflicts with MITM. Disable Tor to continue.
Tor cannot run with domain fronting. Disable Tor to continue.
MITM “Capture all apps” is active. Disable it to enable Tor whole-device tunnel.
Tor whole-device tunnel requires the Xray core
Add a custom root CA to the system trust store. Android shows its own consent screen (and may require a device PIN); on Android 14+ this is the only supported install path.
Included in auto and bulk refresh
Browse a public, open-source pool of free servers. Add any of them right away, or run a quick scan to find the fastest ones. Nothing is added automatically.
In your list · from a subscription
Pull down to load free servers.
Dial this server through another one (this -> front -> internet).
Share the tunnel as a proxy
Export settings & servers to a file
This JSON file contains server credentials, private keys, settings, and subscription URLs without encryption. Save it only somewhere you trust.
Only restore a backup you trust. It can replace settings and import credentials and subscription URLs.
This share URI contains credentials that can grant access to the server. Send it only to someone you trust.
Enable Always-on VPN and Block connections without VPN in Android VPN settings
Always-on VPN is an Android system setting. This opens Android VPN settings so you can enable it.
Blocking connections without VPN (lockdown) is an Android system setting. This opens Android VPN settings.
Pick the fastest server when you tap connect
Reconnect the last server after the device restarts
Allow the app to ignore battery optimization so the tunnel stays connected in the background.
Route 100% of traffic; ignore per-app rules
Carry multiple streams over one connection
Expose the proxy on 0.0.0.0 for LAN clients
Your support helps us build a better app for everyone.
Route your traffic through Cloudflare\'s WARP (WireGuard). Register a free identity automatically, then use it as your outbound or chain your server through it.
Advanced: paste an existing WARP/WireGuard identity instead of registering.
A simple, powerful and modern\nV2Ray client for Android.
Share this device\'s tunnel with other devices on your Wi-Fi or hotspot. Turn sharing on, then configure the other device\'s proxy with the address below.
Reconnect the VPN to apply LAN sharing
LAN sharing works only with the Xray core. Other cores keep the proxy on 127.0.0.1. Set the default core to Xray in VPN settings.
Connect the other device to the same Wi-Fi, or to this phone\'s hotspot.
On that device, open its Wi-Fi proxy settings (or app proxy).
Set a manual SOCKS5 or HTTP proxy to the address above.
Keep this VPN connected while sharing.
Note: fully transparent tethering (no per-device setup) requires root, which Android does not allow for regular apps. This proxy method is the supported alternative.
Follow our Telegram channel and subscribe on YouTube for free configs, updates and support.
Each option turns on real settings or queues a downloadable pack. Change anytime in Settings / Core Manager.
Queues the Tor pack if not bundled. Enable Tor later in Tools — not started automatically.
Marks Domain Fronting tools for you. No download; install the local CA when you use MITM.
Browse privately from a built-in proxied browser tab.
Queues the ByeDPI pack used by SNI / desync bypass in Advanced Tools.
Turns on LAN sharing so SOCKS/HTTP bind on 0.0.0.0 for hotspot clients.
Downloads sing-box and Mihomo (Clash Meta) so you can switch engines in Core Manager.
Crashlytics, Performance, and Analytics stay on for stability. Never hosts, bridges, or subscription URLs.
A modern VLESS, VMess, Trojan and Shadowsocks client for Android.
Xray is included. Extra cores and packs download only if you ask for them.
Route device traffic through Android VPN with the bundled Xray core.
Import VLESS, VMess, Trojan and Shadowsocks. Optional engines handle WireGuard/SSH after download.
Tor, Domain Fronting, ByeDPI, routing and diagnostics — enable or download only what you need.
Please read and accept before continuing.

V2RayEz is a client for user-provided proxy configurations. You are responsible for the servers and configs you add, and for complying with applicable laws.

The app processes connection settings and traffic locally on your device. There is no warranty of uninterrupted service, fitness for a particular purpose, or protection against misuse of third-party servers.

Connecting requires Android VPN permission, which you grant through the system dialog when you first connect. Notifications are optional and used for connection status.

By accepting, you confirm that you understand these terms and will use the app responsibly.
We do not sell destination data. Diagnostics never include hosts, bridges, or subscription URLs.
You are responsible for complying with local laws when using network tools.
I accept the terms of use
Allow notifications so V2RayEz can show connection status and important tunnel alerts.
Selected packs start downloading in the background. Add a server and connect when ready — VPN permission is asked on first connect.
Progress continues in Core Manager. Connect a server if downloads fail on clearnet.
Tunnel started but has no internet — check server or DPI settings
Tor cannot be combined with domain fronting because the fronting dialer connects outside Tor. Turn off one feature.
VPN permission is broken for this install. Uninstall V2RayEz, reboot, then install again (do not use Dual App / app clone).
ByeDPI enabled but libbyedpi.so is missing
%1$s binary is not available on this device
Tor tunnel requires Xray; using Xray instead of %1$s
%1$s needs the sing-box core — install it in Core manager
%1$s pack is not installed — download it in Core manager
%1$s failed to start — check the profile and try again
%1$s is Xray-only — switch core to Xray or disable it
In-app AAR (tunnel). Downloaded Xray builds are not used for VPN.
Changes apply after reconnecting the VPN
Leave default to auto-resolve the server host/SNI on connect. Overriding with an unrelated Cloudflare IP usually causes TLS handshake alerts.
MITM domain fronting isn\'t set up
Generate and install the local certificate to browse fronted sites here.
Start MITM Domain Fronting to route this browser
Only http and https links are allowed
No browser available for this link
Send recent logs and a diagnostics snapshot (scrubbed)
Firebase could not send the bug report
Intercepts HTTPS locally with an on-device CA, then re-establishes each connection with a fronted SNI. Requires installing the local certificate.
1. V2RayEz generates a private root certificate on this device.
2. Once trusted, it decrypts HTTPS locally and rewrites the SNI/Host per your rules.
3. The connection to the real server stays fully encrypted end-to-end.
Tries the system installer; on Android 11+ use Save + Settings if the OS blocks in-app install
Android 11+ blocks one-tap CA install. We open the certificate or Settings — you pick CA certificate and select V2RayEz-MITM-CA.crt from Downloads.
Firefox uses its own certificate store. Open Firefox → Settings → Privacy & Security → View Certificates → Authorities → Import, and pick the exported .crt file too.
Could not start the installer — save to Downloads and install from Settings
Generate the certificate, install it, then confirm it\'s installed before enabling.
Generate the certificate, install it, then confirm it\'s installed before tunneling the device.
Could not save to Downloads — use Share certificate instead
Paste both the certificate and the private key
V2RayEz creates a private root certificate on this device. The private key never leaves your phone.
Save or open the certificate file
Android 11+ blocks in-app CA install. Tap Install to open the cert, or Save to Downloads (%1$s).
Settings → Security → Encryption & credentials → Install a certificate → CA certificate. Pick %1$s (or the .cer file). Confirm with your PIN/pattern.
Android asks for your PIN/pattern/biometric, then shows \"%1$s\". Confirm to add it.
Settings → Security → Trusted credentials → USER should list \"%1$s\".
Back in Domain Fronting, check \"I\'ve installed it\", then enable fronting.
Trust the same CA on a desktop Xray/v2rayN client so it fronts traffic the same way.
Contains the private key — only share it to a PC/device you control.
Only import a CA you generated yourself — this replaces the current on-device certificate.
Requires the certificate to be installed and confirmed
One rule per line: domain = front-domain. Hosts not listed go direct (no MITM).
Unmatched hosts always exit direct while MITM is on. Only domains in the rules above are fronted.
Requires VPN permission. Routes all apps through MITM (Xray only). Conflicts with Tor whole-device. Unmatched hosts still exit direct.
Use “Run local MITM proxy” for Browser only. Use “Tunnel whole device” for VpnService capture of every app.

HTTP/3 (QUIC) is blocked so traffic uses TLS MITM. Install the user CA. Apps that pin certificates (stock YouTube) may still fail — use Chrome or the in-app Browser to verify YouTube fronting. Reset rules to defaults after updating.
Starts SOCKS + HTTP on this device (no VPN). Powers the in-app Browser.
Xray is already running (stop VPN or other core first)
Proxy started but local ports are not accepting connections
Turn off Tor “Tunnel whole device” before enabling MITM capture
Install and acknowledge the Domain Fronting CA before connecting
Domain Fronting requires the Xray core
